things.name: Robert Burns House Museum
things.description: The house where Scotland's national poet spent his last years — original manuscripts, portraits and personal artefacts in a free museum

things.name: Robert Burns Centre
things.description: Burns museum in an 18th-century watermill on the Nith — an excellent introduction to Dumfries' rich Burns heritage

things.name: Caerlaverock Castle
things.description: Unique triangular medieval castle rising from a moat on the Solway shore — one of Scotland's most romantic and picturesque fortresses

things.name: Sweetheart Abbey (New Abbey)
things.description: Romantic ruined Cistercian abbey founded in 1273 by Devorgilla of Galloway — named for the embalmed heart of her husband she carried with her

things.name: Globe Inn (Burns' Local)
things.description: Robert Burns' favourite pub since 1610 — still serving drinkers today, with Burns' chair and the howff where he drank and composed verses

things.name: Dumfries Museum & Camera Obscura
things.description: Fascinating local museum in a Victorian windmill tower topped by Scotland's oldest working camera obscura with live views of the town

things.name: Drumlanrig Castle
things.description: Spectacular pink sandstone castle of the Dukes of Buccleuch with a priceless art collection — Rembrandt, Holbein and the original 'Da Vinci bicycle'

things.name: Galloway Forest Park (Dark Sky Park)
things.description: Britain's first Dark Sky Park and one of the largest forests in the UK — superb stargazing, mountain biking and red squirrel spotting

things.name: Ruthwell Cross (Anglian Runic Stone)
things.description: One of the finest surviving early Christian carved crosses in Europe (c. AD 700) — inscribed with the oldest known poem in Old English

things.name: Solway Firth Coastal Walk (Caerlaverock WWT)
things.description: WWT Caerlaverock wetland centre on the Solway shore — home to tens of thousands of barnacle geese wintering from Svalbard each autumn
